latest.content:
7 Apr 2026 08:31
RNS Number : 4435Z
Trustpilot Group PLC
07 April 2026
7
April
202
6
TRUSTPILOT GROUP PLC
Notification of Transactions by Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities ("PDMRs") and Persons Closely Associated with them
Grant of Long Term Incentive Plan and
Deferred Share Bonus Plan Awards
Vesting of 2023 Deferred Share Bonus Awards
Long Term Incentive Plan Award
Trustpilot Group plc (the "
Company
") announces that on
1
April 202
6,
the following award over ordinary shares of £0.01 each in the Company was granted under the Trustpilot Group plc Long Term Incentive Plan (the
"
LTIP
"
) to the following person discharging managerial responsibilities:
Participant
Position
No. of Shares under Award
Adrian Blair
Chief Executive Officer
901,634
The award was granted as a conditional award and will ordinarily vest on 1
April
2029, subject to the grantee's continued service and the Remuneration Committee's assessment of the extent to which the award's performance conditions are satisfied.
The vesting of 75 per cent. of such award (the "
Relative TSR Part
") is subject to the Company's total shareholder return ("
TSR
") performance over a three-year period that commenced on 1
April
202
6
relative to the TSR performance over the same period of the constituents of the FTSE 250 Index (excluding investment trusts and the Company) as at 1
April
202
6
.
One quarter of the Relative TSR Part will vest for median ranking performance, rising on a straight
-
line basis to full vesting of the Relative TSR Part for upper quartile ranking (or better) relative TSR performance.
The vesting of 25 per cent. of such award (the "
EPS Part
") is subject to targets set for growth in adjusted earnings per share ("
Adjusted EPS
"). One quarter of the
EPS
Part will vest for
an Adjusted EPS over the measurement period of 30%
compound annual growth rate ("CAGR")
, rising on a straight-line basis to full vesting of the EPS Part
for
an Adjusted EPS over the measurement period of 45% CAGR (or better)
.
As an additional condition, a term of the performance conditions include that no part of such LTIP award will vest unless the Remuneration Committee is satisfied as to overall Company performance over the period until vesting, and, as required by the UK Corporate Governance Code, the Remuneration Committee will retain a power to moderate the vesting level of the award if this is appropriate in all of the circumstances, including consideration of shareholder experience.
The award is subject to a two-year post-vesting holding period.
The following notifications, made pursuant to Article 19 of the UK Market Abuse Regulation, provide further details.

Deferred Share Bonus Plan Awards
The Company also announces that on 1 April 2026, conditional awards over ordinary shares of £0.01 each in the Company were made under the Trustpilot Group plc Deferred Share Bonus Plan ("DSBP") to the persons discharging managerial responsibilities:
PDMR

The DSBP awards relates to the deferral of 25% of the bonus achieved for financial year 2025 being awarded as a DSBP award for such individuals.
The vesting date for the DSBP awards will normally be two years from the date of award and the awards will be settled by the transfer of existing ordinary shares of the Company (other than treasury shares).
For both the LTIP and the DSBP, the number of shares under the awards was calculated using the three-month average share price to 1 April 2026, being the date of Remuneration Committee approval of the grants.

RNS Number : 9158Y
Trustpilot Group PLC
31 March 2026
31 March 2026
Trustpilot Group plc
(the "Company")
Total voting rights
The following information is disclosed in accordance with DTR 5.6.1R of the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules of the Financial Conduct Authority ("
FCA
").
As at the date of this announcement, the Company's issued ordinary share capital consists of 387,492,638 ordinary shares of £0.01 each (the "
Ordinary Shares
"), each carrying the right to one vote at a general meeting of the Company. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury.
Therefore, the total number of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares is 387,492,638.
The figure in the preceding sentence may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Ordinary Shares under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

company_30s.what_they_do: Trustpilot runs a global online review platform that lets consumers post and read product, service and business ratings. The core product is a searchable database of millions of reviews, and the company monetises through subscription licences for businesses and premium services for consumers.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The review ecosystem is a key source of consumer insight for brands and shoppers alike. Trustpilot’s data drives marketing decisions, product improvements and online reputation management, making it a critical player in the e‑commerce and digital‑marketing space. Its scale and brand recognition give it a competitive edge over newer, niche review sites.
company_30s.current_state: The firm has moved from a growth‑phase focus to a stable, cash‑generating operation. Recent quarterly results beat expectations, a share‑buyback has been launched, and a new CFO has joined the board. These moves suggest management is tightening governance, returning value to shareholders and preparing for a more disciplined growth path. The share price sits well below its all‑time peak, indicating a potential valuation gap.

rns_narrative: Governance refresh: a new CFO, director shareholding changes and a transaction in own shares all point to a board that is tightening control and signalling confidence in the business.
Financial optimisation: a secondary placing and a share‑buyback programme demonstrate a willingness to return capital to investors while still raising funds for future initiatives.
Regulatory focus: the appeal of an AGCM finding shows the company is actively defending its market position and seeking to reduce any potential legal drag on growth.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – traders are quietly watching the buy‑back and regulatory outcome for a potential rebound.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few active voices are lining up for a second bite after the buy‑back and CFO appointment, arguing that the price is now a bargain compared to the company’s long‑term value.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: A handful of traders warn that the regulatory appeal could still hurt the brand and that the share price may stay stuck if the buy‑back stalls.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The conversation has moved from neutral to mildly bullish as the buy‑back announcement spurred a few new long positions.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – there’s no mainstream chatter, which is a positive sign for a contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the share‑buyback – a clear sign that the company is willing to pay its own shares and will likely lift the price.
Outcome of the AGCM appeal – a favorable ruling could remove a regulatory cloud and lift sentiment.
Quarterly earnings beat – a positive surprise on profitability will reinforce the narrative that the business is now profitable.
New partnership or product launch – any announcement that expands the revenue mix could trigger a jump.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that coincides with the buy‑back announcement, the stock touching the 200p support level, and a clear sign that the regulatory appeal is resolved. Any of these will be a confirmation cue.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price is still in the early stages of a crash‑recovery cycle. APEX indications suggest a tight squeeze, so the next meaningful move is likely to come soon once the buy‑back and regulatory outcomes are clear.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share has fallen sharply, likely below 70‑95 % of its peak, which historically creates a powerful mean‑reversion engine. A deep crash combined with a buy‑back and regulatory win can unleash a 150–200 % upside if the price climbs to the mid‑400p range – a reward that far exceeds the limited downside risk.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Regulatory uncertainty remains, and if the AGCM appeal is denied or the buy‑back stalls, the price could stay trapped. In that worst‑case scenario the share could fall another 20‑30 % before stabilising.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: down to 100p (≈ 50 % from the current 200p level). Potential gain: up to 600p (≈ 200 % from 200p). Risk‑reward ratio roughly 1 : 4, favouring a long stance.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter around 200p after a volume surge that confirms the buy‑back completion. Use a stop 50p below that level (≈ 150p). Target 400p, then 600p if momentum holds.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the buy‑back is announced and volume spikes. Watch the price hit the 200p support; if it stays above that for 3‑5 trading days, consider a small entry.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – no imminent delisting or bankruptcy filings.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With a deep crash, a timely buy‑back and a pending regulatory appeal, the stock offers a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play that could deliver 200 % upside if the mean‑reversion kicks in.
